file_insights.changes_summary: Added cursor positioning and minimal redraw logic with two new utility functions and refactored list_cards_watch() event loop

functions_added.name: count_display_lines
functions_added.purpose: Calculate total output lines for cursor positioning
functions_added.complexity: O(n) filesystem traversal across states and team directories

functions_added.name: count_state_group_lines
functions_added.purpose: Count lines for single state group (header + cards + blank line)
functions_added.complexity: O(n) where n is card count in state directory

functions_modified.name: list_cards_watch
key_changes: Replaced full-screen clear (\x1b[2J\x1b[H) with cursor-up repositioning (\x1b[{}A)
Added CardStats tracking (prev_stats) for change detection
Added line count tracking (prev_line_count) for cursor positioning
Implements stats-based minimal redraw: only redraw if total or by_state changed
Re-queries terminal width on every redraw (explicitly not cached)

patterns_discovered.description: Compares current CardStats with previous stats; only triggers redraw if total count or per-state counts differ
patterns_discovered.code_evidence: stats_changed = prev_stats.as_ref().map(|prev| prev.total != current_stats.total || prev.by_state != current_stats.by_state).unwrap_or(true)

patterns_discovered.description: Uses ANSI escape \x1b[{}A to move cursor up N lines instead of clearing entire screen, then rewrites output

patterns_discovered.pattern: Dynamic terminal width on every redraw
patterns_discovered.description: Calls util::term_width() per redraw cycle with explicit comment 'do not cache' to handle terminal resize events
Maintains both prev_stats (for change detection) and prev_line_count (for next cursor positioning) across loop iterations
count_display_lines() handles root state directories and team-* subdirectories with sorted iteration
patterns_discovered.code_evidence: lines iterate states, within each state scan root + all team-* dirs, sort team_dirs for consistent ordering

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Terminal width must not be cached
gotchas_discovered.risk: Previous versions may have cached term_width, causing display corruption on terminal resize during watch loop
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Explicit call with comment 'do not cache' on every redraw; variable bound as _term_width (possible side-effect usage)

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Cursor-up assumes no scrollback during partial redraw
gotchas_discovered.risk: If list_cards() output is shorter than previous frame, orphaned lines remain visible below output
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Relies on list_cards() overwriting all previous lines; no explicit clearing of extra lines if output shrinks

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Stats equality check must handle first render
gotchas_discovered.risk: unwrap_or(true) ensures first render always happens, but if prev_stats is None on first event, logic may be subtle
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Pattern .map().unwrap_or(true) correctly forces redraw when prev_stats is None

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Terminal width variable unused in code
gotchas_discovered.risk: _term_width is queried but not stored or used; may indicate unfinished refactor or future feature
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Named with underscore prefix; verify if side effects are intentional or if variable should be used

gotchas_discovered.risk: If prev_line_count exceeds terminal height, cursor moves past top of visible area into scrollback
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: None implemented; assumes prev_line_count <= terminal height in practice

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: list_cards() errors do not stop watch loop
gotchas_discovered.risk: If list_cards() fails, display becomes stale; loop continues silently until next stats change
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Error logged to stderr but watch loop continues; no recovery mechanism

approach_outcome.goal_achieved: Cursor positioning and minimal redraw logic implemented in list_cards_watch()
key_improvements: Eliminates full-screen flicker (was \x1b[2J\x1b[H, now incremental cursor-up)
Reduces CPU churn via stats-based change detection (only redraw when cards change)
Handles terminal resize dynamically (re-query width every cycle)
approach_outcome.commit_message: be2efed auto-claude: subtask-1-4 - Implement cursor positioning and minimal redraw logic

recommendations.recommendation: Batch stdout flushes per redraw cycle
recommendations.rationale: Current code has separate flush after cursor-up; could defer to single flush after list_cards() completes
recommendations.implementation: Move all print!() calls before flush, or use BufWriter wrapper

recommendations.recommendation: Cache count_display_lines result when stats unchanged
recommendations.rationale: count_display_lines and count_state_group_lines do full directory scans on every event; if stats are identical, line count will be identical
recommendations.implementation: Cache line count result, invalidate only when stats change

recommendations.rationale: Cursor-up logic has no safety check; if prev_line_count > terminal height, cursor goes into scrollback
recommendations.implementation: Clamp prev_line_count to min(term_height - 1, prev_line_count) before cursor-up

recommendations.rationale: Variable is queried but never used; unclear if this is intentional (side effects) or incomplete refactor
recommendations.implementation: Add doc comment explaining if this is for side effects, or use the value in output logic

recommendations.recommendation: Add recovery mechanism for stale display on list_cards() error
recommendations.rationale: If list_cards() fails, display becomes stale; loop continues until next stats change, which may be long
recommendations.implementation: On error, force full redraw (clear + reprint) as fallback, or trigger stats recalc to detect change

recommendations.recommendation: Test with rapid terminal resizes during watch loop
recommendations.rationale: Term width re-query logic is correct but behavior under rapid resize stress is untested
recommendations.implementation: Add integration test that spawns watch loop and programmatically resizes pty
